title: Of Elaborate Dressing of the Hair in Other Ways, and Its Bearing Upon Salvation.

source.title: ANF04. Fathers of the Third Century: Tertullian, Part Fourth; Minucius Felix; Commodian; Origen, Parts First and Second

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: What service, again, does all the labour spent in arranging the hair render to salvation?

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Why is no rest allowed to your hair, which must now be bound, now loosed, now cultivated, now thinned out?

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Some are anxious to force their hair into curls, some to let it hang loose and flying; not with good simplicity: beside which, you affix I know not what enormities of subtle and textile perukes; now, after the manner of a helmet of undressed hide, as it were a sheath for the head and a covering for the crown; now, a mass (drawn) backward toward the neck.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: The wonder is, that there is no (open) contending against the Lord’s prescripts!

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: It has been pronounced that no one can add to his own stature.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: You, however, do add to your weight some kind of rolls, or shield-bosses, to be piled upon your necks!

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: If you feel no shame at the enormity, feel some at the pollution; for fear you may be fitting on a holy and Christian head the slough of some one else’s head, unclean perchance, guilty perchance and destined to hell.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Nay, rather banish quite away from your “free” head all this slavery of ornamentation.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: In vain do you labour to seem adorned: in vain do you call in the aid of all the most skilful manufacturers of false hair.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: I believe (He does so) for fear the heads of some should be seen!

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: And oh that in “that day” of Christian exultation, I, most miserable (as I am), may elevate my head, even though below (the level of) your heels!

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: I shall (then) see whether you will rise with (your) ceruse and rouge and saffron, and in all that parade of headgear: whether it will be women thus tricked out whom the angels carry up to meet Christ in the air!

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: If these (decorations) are now good, and of God, they will then also present themselves to the rising bodies, and will recognise their several places.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: But nothing can rise except flesh and spirit sole and pure.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Whatever, therefore, does not rise in (the form of) spirit and flesh is condemned, because it is not of God.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: From things which are condemned abstain, even at the present day.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: At the present day let God see you such as He will see you then.
